relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Apostles are attendants of Messiah and stewards of Divine mysteries.
Stewardship asks first for trustworthiness.
Human examination is not Paul’s final mirror.
A clear conscience is not final justification; the Lord examines.
Do not judge before the season; hidden heart-counsels will be brought to light.
Paul and Apollos become examples so the community stops puffing one against another.
Everything is received, so boasting is illusion.
Corinth feels full and royal too soon.
The apostles appear last, exposed before world, messengers, and humans.
The community’s self-image contrasts sharply with apostolic weakness.
Apostolic life includes hunger, thirst, poor clothing, blows, and homelessness.
Working hands bless under insult and endure pursuit.
Slandered, they appeal; treated as refuse, they continue.
Paul writes not to shame but to admonish beloved children.
Many guardians may exist, but Paul claims fatherly origin through the good news.
Imitation is urged as embodied teaching.
Timothy will remind them of Paul’s ways in Messiah.
Some puff up as if Paul will not come.
Paul will test not inflated speech but power.
The Divine reign is not performance-word but power.
Correction can arrive as rod or as love with gentle spirit.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 4 attacks spiritual ego without attacking the person. The medicine is trustworthy stewardship, hidden-heart humility, and power made real through love.
